trade.summary: Canada exports aerospace products, agricultural goods, and technology to the UAE. The UAE exports refined petroleum and chemicals. Trade volume is moderate and has grown since 2020.

diplomatic.summary: Relations have been complicated by a 2021 diplomatic spat when the UAE restricted Canadian overflights and flights, partly linked to Canadian criticism of UAE human rights practices and the Al Jazeera dispute. Relations partially recovered but remain cautious.
recent_incidents: 2021 UAE flight restrictions on Canadian airlines retaliatory measures

military.summary: Canada participated in Gulf security operations and has sold defense equipment to the UAE in the past. However political tensions have limited military cooperation compared to other Five Eyes partners.

financial.summary: UAE sovereign wealth funds have invested in Canadian real estate and financial assets. Canadian pension funds (CPPIB, OMERS) have made significant infrastructure investments in the UAE. Financial ties remain strong despite political friction.

energy.summary: Canada is a major oil and gas producer but exports primarily to the US market. Direct energy trade with the UAE is limited. Both compete in global LNG markets.

scientific.summary: Research collaboration exists in healthcare, AI, and sustainability through university partnerships. Several Canadian universities have established UAE campuses or partnerships.
